texto: I would rather trust my child to a serpent than to a teacher who does not believe in God.
explicacao: Citação de Karl G. Maeser sobre temas ligados a educacao. “I would rather trust my child to a serpent than to a teacher who does not believe in God.” articula educacao, trust, god em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como god e child.

texto: I have been asked what I mean by “word of honor.” I will tell you. Place me behind prison walls—walls of stone ever so high, ever so thick, reaching ever so far into the ground—there is a possibility that in some way or another I might be able to escape; but stand me on the floor and draw a chalk line around me and have me give my word of honor never to cross it. Can I get out of that circle? No, never! I’d die first.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Karl G. Maeser que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “I have been asked what I mean by “word of honor.” I will tell you. Place me behind prison walls—w…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, byu, honor, com foco em honor.

frase_original: I have been asked what I mean by “word of honor.” I will tell you. Place me behind prison walls—walls of stone ever so high, ever so thick, reaching ever so far into the ground—there is a possibility that in some way or another I might be able to escape; but stand me on the floor and draw a chalk line around me and have me give my word of honor never to cross it. Can I get out of that circle? No, never! I’d die first.

texto: Every human being is a world in miniature. It has its own centre of observation, its own way of forming concepts and of arriving at conclusions, its own degree of sensibility, its own life's work to do, and its own destiny to reach. All these features may be encompassed by general conditions, governed by general laws, and subject to unforeseen influences and incidents, but within the sphere of their own activity, they constitute that great principle which we call individuality.
explicacao: Citação de Karl G. Maeser sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Every human being is a world in miniature. frase_original: Every human being is a world in miniature. It has its own centre of observation, its own way of forming concepts and of arriving at conclusions, its own degree of sensibility, its own life's work to do, and its own destiny to reach. All these features may be encompassed by general conditions, governed by general laws, and subject to unforeseen influences and incidents, but within the sphere of their own activity, they constitute that great principle which we call individuality.
